User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the IDGenius application, including their personal information and Star balance for the monetization system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and notifications.

currentStarsBalance.description: The current number of Stars available for the user to spend.

totalStarsEarned.description: The cumulative total of Stars the user has earned through free actions.

totalStarsPurchased.description: The cumulative total of Stars the user has acquired through purchases.

totalStarsUsed.description: The cumulative total of Stars the user has spent on premium features.

lastLoginTimestamp.description: The timestamp of the user's last successful login, used for calculating login streaks.

consecutiveLoginDays.description: The number of consecutive days the user has logged in, used for awarding streak bonuses.

referralCode.description: A unique code assigned to the user, which can be shared to refer new users.

referredByUserId.description: Reference to the User who referred this user to the application. (Relationship: User 1:N User)

isProfileComplete.description: Indicates whether the user has completed all required profile information, a condition for earning Stars.

IDTemplate.title: IDTemplate
IDTemplate.description: Defines the structure, layout, and appearance of different ID types available for generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IDTemplate entity.

name.description: The display name of the ID template (e.g., 'Student ID', 'Employee Badge', 'Membership Card').

description.description: A detailed description of the template and its intended use.

templateSchemaJson.description: A JSON string containing the schema or configuration defining the fields, layout, and styling of the template.

previewImageUrl.description: URL to an image providing a visual preview of the template.

starCost.description: The number of Stars required to use this premium template. If 0, the template is free.

GeneratedID.title: GeneratedID
GeneratedID.description: Represents a specific ID created by a user based on an IDTemplate, including all entered data and generated assets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedID entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated this ID. (Relationship: User 1:N GeneratedID)

templateId.description: Reference to the IDTemplate used to create this ID. (Relationship: IDTemplate 1:N GeneratedID)

inputDataJson.description: A JSON string containing all the user-provided data fields for this specific ID instance.

photoUrl.description: URL to the AI-verified and processed photo incorporated into the ID.

qrCodeData.description: The raw data encoded within the QR code generated for this ID.

downloadLink.description: URL to the final downloadable image or PDF file of the generated ID. This may be null if the ID is not yet finalized or exported.

status.description: The current status of the generated ID (e.g., 'draft', 'finalized', 'downloaded', 'archived').

createdAt.description: The timestamp when this ID was first created.

lastModifiedAt.description: The timestamp when this ID was last modified.

starCostApplied.description: The total number of Stars, if any, deducted for using premium features during the creation or export of this ID.

StarPackage.title: StarPackage
StarPackage.description: Defines the different packages of Stars that users can purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StarPackage entity.

name.description: The name of the Star package (e.g., 'Starter', 'Standard', 'Pro', 'Business').

starsAmount.description: The total number of Stars included in this package.

priceCDF.description: The price of the package in Congolese Francs (CDF).

priceUSD.description: The approximate equivalent price of the package in US Dollars (USD) for reference.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if this package should be highlighted in the UI (e.g., 'Most Popular').

orderIndex.description: A numerical value to determine the display order of the packages in the user interface.

MobileMoneyOperator.title: MobileMoneyOperator
MobileMoneyOperator.description: Represents a mobile money service provider through which users can purchase Stars.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MobileMoneyOperator entity.

name.description: The name of the mobile money operator (e.g., 'Airtel Money', 'Orange Money', 'M-PESA').

countryCode.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code where this operator primarily functions (e.g., 'CD' for Congo-DRC).

displayColor.description: A color code (e.g., hex code or name) associated with the operator for UI branding.

logoUrl.description: URL to the official logo of the mobile money operator.

StarTransaction.title: StarTransaction
StarTransaction.description: Records every instance where a user's Star balance changes, detailing gains, purchases, and expenditures.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StarTransaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User involved in this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N StarTransaction)

type.description: The category of the transaction: 'purchase' (Stars bought), 'earned' (Stars obtained freely), or 'spent' (Stars consumed for features).

starsChange.description: The amount of Stars changed by this transaction (positive for gains/purchases, negative for expenditures).

balanceAfterTransaction.description: The user's Star balance immediately after this transaction occurred.

description.description: A human-readable description of the transaction (e.g., 'Purchased Standard Star Pack', 'Earned for referring a friend', 'Spent 5 stars on Premium Photo AI Enhancement').

timestamp.description: The date and time when the transaction took place.

relatedObjectId.description: Optional: Reference to the ID of an associated entity (e.g., StarPackage ID if purchased, GeneratedID if stars were spent on an ID creation). Leave null if not applicable.

relatedObjectType.description: Optional: Indicates the type of the 'relatedObjectId' (e.g., 'StarPackage', 'GeneratedID', 'StarEarningActionType', 'StarSpendingFeatureType'). Leave null if not applicable.

StarEarningActionType.title: StarEarningActionType
StarEarningActionType.description: Defines the different types of actions users can perform to earn free Stars.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StarEarningActionType entity.

name.description: The name of the earning action (e.g., 'Refer a Friend', '7-Day Login Streak', 'Rate the App', 'Complete Profile', 'Watch an Ad', 'Contribute AI Photo Feedback').

starsAwarded.description: The number of Stars awarded for completing this action.

description.description: A detailed explanation of the action and its benefits to the user and the app.

maxOccurrencesPerUser.description: The maximum number of times a user can perform this action to earn stars (e.g., 1 for 'Complete Profile', potentially unlimited for 'Watch an Ad').

cooldownSeconds.description: The cooldown period in seconds before a user can perform this action again to earn stars (e.g., for 'Watch an Ad').

StarSpendingFeatureType.title: StarSpendingFeatureType
StarSpendingFeatureType.description: Defines the different premium features or actions within the app that require Stars to use.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StarSpendingFeatureType entity.

name.description: The name of the feature that costs Stars (e.g., 'Premium Photo AI Enhancement', 'Access Premium ID Template', 'Use AI ID Assistant', 'Advanced ID Security Features', 'Temporary Unlimited Downloads/Shares', 'High-Resolution/Batch Export').

starsCost.description: The number of Stars required to use this feature.

description.description: A detailed explanation of the premium feature and its value to the user.

definition.description: Stores user profiles and their cumulative star statistics. Only the authenticated user can read/write their own user document. This document is central for star balance management.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual ID documents generated by a user. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence, ensuring only the owner can access their generated IDs.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this generated ID.

params.name: generatedId
params.description: The unique identifier of the generated ID document.

definition.description: Logs all Star activities (gains, purchases, spending) for a user. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence, ensuring only the owner can access their transaction history.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with this Star transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Star transaction record.

definition.description: A collection used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that the corresponding `userId` has administrative privileges. No content beyond the document ID is strictly required, but it typically points to a user's ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds an administrative role.

definition.description: Stores pre-defined ID templates available in the application. These are globally readable by all authenticated users but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ID template.

definition.description: Defines the different purchasable Star packages. These are globally readable by all authenticated users but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: packageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Star package.

definition.description: Lists the available Mobile Money payment operators in Congo-DRC. These are globally readable by all authenticated users but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: operatorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Mobile Money operator.

definition.description: Defines the types of actions users can perform to earn free Stars. These are globally readable by all authenticated users but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: actionTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Star earning action type.

definition.description: Defines the premium features or actions within the app that require Stars to use. These are globally readable by all authenticated users but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: featureTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Star spending feature type.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for IDGenius is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, heavily prioritizing Authorization Independence and QAP (Query as Permission) compliance, especially with the introduction of the 'Stars' monetization system. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   User-specific data such as `GeneratedID` and `StarTransaction` documents include the `userId` field directly within each document. This critical denormalization allows Firestore security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent `User` document. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations (like creating a `GeneratedID` and its corresponding `StarTransaction` in a single batch) and simplifying rule logic significantly. Any access to a user's generated IDs or transaction history is self-contained within the document's data and the path. 
*   The `User` document itself, located at `/users/{userId}`, is the central hub for the user's Star balance (`currentStarsBalance`, `totalStarsEarned`, etc.). This means that any rule checking a user's ability to spend stars or tracking their overall progress can directly reference fields within this document, or the document of the specific `GeneratedID`/`StarTransaction` containing its owner's UID. This avoids needing to fetch data from another, potentially unauthorized, path.

**QAPs (Query as Permission) for Secure Listing:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** For user-owned data like `GeneratedID`s and `StarTransaction`s, the structure uses hierarchical paths: `/users/{userId}/generated_ids/{generatedId}` and `/users/{userId}/star_transactions/{transactionId}`. This design intrinsically satisfies QAPs because a user can only perform `list` operations (queries) within their specific `/users/{userId}/` subtree. Firestore rules can enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, ensuring that a user can only query their own data. This prevents unauthorized users from listing other users' sensitive information and simplifies rule writing as no complex filtering is required within the rules themselves. The query implicitly defines the allowed permission.
*   **Structural Segregation for Public/Admin Data:** Global, static data entities such as `IDTemplate`, `StarPackage`, `MobileMoneyOperator`, `StarEarningActionType`, and `StarSpendingFeatureType` are stored in top-level collections (e.g., `/id_templates`, `/star_packages`). These collections have a homogeneous security posture: they are generally readable by all authenticated users for display in the UI (e.g., showing available ID templates or star packages) and are writable only by administrators. By segregating these into separate collections, the security rules for these paths are straightforward (e.g., `allow read: if request.auth.uid != null; allow write: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid));`). This ensures that list queries on these collections are secure by design, as the data is intended for broad consumption (read) or restricted management (write) without complex row-level filtering based on user identity for reads.

**Overall Design:**
*   **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** Administrative roles are managed through a dedicated collection (`/admin_roles/{userId}`). The mere existence of a document at this path grants admin privileges to the `userId`, enabling simple and auditable role management without custom claims.
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** The `GeneratedID` entity includes a `status` field for clear state management, rather than inferring it from multiple flags.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Wildcards (`{userId}`, `{templateId}`) are descriptive, and authorization fields (like `userId`) are consistently named across relevant entities. This enhances debuggability and maintainability of security rules.

This structure ensures that authorization rules are simple, robust, and highly performant, adhering to best practices for Firestore security and scalability.
